source: Petitions. Mr. S. Sinnadurai, formerly class III clerk, Posts and Telegraphs Department__CO273:660:9
filename: Petitions. Mr. S. Sinnadurai, formerly class III clerk, Posts and Telegraphs Department__CO273:660:9.txt
summary:
- **Core topic/purpose:** Petition (memorial) by **S. (Sethunather) Sinnadurai**, formerly a clerk in the **Federated Malay States (FMS) Posts and Telegraphs Department**, seeking a **pension** (requested to run from the termination of his service) after his employment ended in 1918.  
- **Geography/time period:** Service in **Kuala Lumpur** and **Seremban**, **Federated Malay States**; petitioner later residing in **Vaddukoddai, Jaffna, Ceylon**. Events center on **1907–1918** service/termination, with renewed petitions and official handling in **1936** and **1940–1941**.  
- **Key events/decisions (1918):** Joined service **1 Mar 1907**; by 1918 held a **pensionable Class II clerk** post. In **Oct–Nov 1918** he was reported for irregularities and charged under **General Order No. 24**; the **Chief Secretary to the FMS Government** directed **dismissal**.  
- **Modification of penalty:** After a petition dated **29 Nov 1918**, the **High Commissioner** (named in the memorial as **Sir Arthur Young**) judged dismissal “too harsh,” ordered that his **services be terminated** but granted **four months’ salary** as a gratuity and stated the **“stigma of dismissal” was withdrawn**.  
- **Subsequent petitions and record issues:** Further petitions for re-employment or pension in **Feb 1919** and **1920** were rejected; a **1936** petition for pension was also refused, citing that records did not show formal retirement for inefficiency or dismissal, but his prior record included **14 fines and 20 cautions** (contradicting his claim of a clean record).  
- **1940–1941 official conclusion:** The **High Commissioner’s report** found the 1940 petition inaccurate on key points (disciplinary history; opportunity to answer charges). Officials noted no pension entitlement under the law at the time and that any pension now would require a **special vote**; due to the case being about **22 years old**, they recommended **no intervention**.  
- **Outcome communicated:** A despatch signed **(Lord) Lloyd** to **Governor Sir Andrew Caldecott** states the petition was received and considered but the High Commissioner was **“not prepared to intervene”**; recommendation to inform the petitioner accordingly (with references to despatches including **26 Sep 1940** and **27 Nov** [year implied 1940]).
